title: Debug Deployment: Selector Mismatch
description:
Debug and fix a broken deployment manifest that fails to create pods due to a selector mismatch - one of the most common and frustrating Deployment errors.

**Focus**: Understanding the critical immutable relationship between `spec.selector.matchLabels` and `spec.template.metadata.labels`.

This exercise simulates a real-world scenario where a developer accidentally uses inconsistent labels, resulting in a Deployment that appears to exist but creates zero pods. The error message "selector does not match template labels" is Kubernetes preventing an invalid configuration. When labels don't match, the Deployment controller cannot identify which pods it should manage, so it refuses to create any. This validation exists because changing a selector after creation would orphan existing pods, creating management chaos.
steps: Identify the mismatch between selector labels and template labels
Fix the template labels to match selector (app: redis, tier: cache)
Ensure Deployment creates 3 replicas of redis:7-alpine
Apply deployment and confirm pods are created
Test Redis connectivity using redis-cli ping
hints: Labels are case-sensitive and must match exactly
The selector expects tier: cache, but the template has tier: database
Kubernetes won't create pods if the selector doesn't match the template

editor_config.initial_code:
# BROKEN: This deployment has a selector mismatch!
# Your task: Fix it so pods are created successfully

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: redis-cache
  labels:
    app: redis
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis
      tier: cache
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: redis
        tier: database  # ❌ BUG: This doesn't match selector (cache vs database)
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: redis
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
          name: redis

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: redis-cache
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis
      tier: cache
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: redis
        tier: cache  # ✅ FIXED: Now matches selector
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: redis
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 6379
          name: redis
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Apply the deployment
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for deployment to be ready
timeout=90
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  ready=$(kubectl get deployment redis-cache -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  if [ "$ready" = "3" ]; then
    echo "✓ Deployment has 3 ready replicas"
    
    # Verify pod labels match selector
    pod_count=$(kubectl get pods -l app=redis,tier=cache --no-headers | wc -l)
    if [ "$pod_count" -eq "3" ]; then
      echo "✓ Pod labels correctly match selector"
      
      # Test Redis connectivity
      pod_name=$(kubectl get pods -l app=redis,tier=cache -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
      response=$(kubectl exec "$pod_name" -- redis-cli ping 2>/dev/null)
      
      if [ "$response" = "PONG" ]; then
        echo "✓ Redis is running and responding to commands"
        exit 0
      fi
    fi
  fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done

echo "✗ Deployment failed to create pods or selector mismatch still exists"
kubectl get deployment redis-cache
kubectl describe deployment redis-cache | tail -10
exit 1
